articles.headline: 2026 World Cup: Which 48 teams qualified for the tournament?
articles.description: The race to qualify for the 2026 FIFA World Cup is complete. Here's how it all went down across the globe.

images.name: Marcotti: Italy have 'no excuses' for another World Cup failure
images.caption: Gab Marcotti reacts to Italy failing to qualify for a third straight World Cup after losing to Bosnia and Herzegovina on penalties.

articles.headline: Could Morocco beat Brazil in Group C at the World Cup?

articles.description: Adidas has released a huge batch of home kits for 22 countries, many of whom have either qualified or still hope to qualify for the FIFA 2026 World Cup.

article.headline: Liverpool's Nunez scores as Uruguay dump Brazil
article.description: Darwin Nunez and Nicolas de la Cruz scored to give Uruguay a 2-0 win over Brazil in their 2026 World Cup qualifier on Tuesday as Neymar was forced off late in the first half after suffering what appeared to be a serious knee injury.
article.linkText: Liverpool's Nunez scores as Uruguay dump Brazil

images.name: Is Neymar's injury a 'blessing in disguise' for Brazil?
images.caption: Alejandro Moreno reacts to Neymar's torn ACL injury and how it will impact Brazil going forward.

article.story:
Darwin Nunez and Nicolas de la Cruz scored to give Uruguay a 2-0 win over Brazil in their 2026 World Cup qualifier on Tuesday as Neymar was forced off late in the first half after suffering what appeared to be a serious knee injury.

Nunez opened the scoring in the 42nd minute with a diving header, and Brazil's night went from bad to worse two minutes later when Neymar went down after tussling for the ball with De la Cruz.

Visibly distressed, Neymar left the pitch on a stretcher after receiving treatment for several minutes.

Brazil FA sources said the forward had sustained a severe left knee sprain and that he would undergo tests to determine if there was ligament damage.

De la Cruz extended the hosts' lead with a close range effort in the 77th minute, with Nunez providing the assist.

The victory lifted Uruguay to second in the CONMEBOL standings on seven points, level with Brazil and Venezuela.
